EUR 38.8B backlog gives ~2 years revenue visibility
FY2025 FCF EUR 11B on EUR 32.7B revenue; EUR 6B buybacks executed
Disciplined vertical M&A (Cymer, Zeiss stake, HMI); 0.6% SBC

MATCH Act / US-China export controls compressing China mix (36% to 19%)
Multiple compression risk at 30.5x fwd P/E, 43.8x EV/EBITDA, 69.5x P/FCF
High-NA gross margin learning curve must bend to hit 2030 guide
red_flags: Monte Carlo P(IV > Price) = 0.0% over 10,000 simulations
Entire 25-cell sensitivity grid sits below current price
Stock at 91% of 52-week range, +139.8% from April 2025 low
ASML stopped disclosing quarterly order intake in Q1 2026
Terminal value is 76-84% of EV in DCF - highly sensitive to assumptions

Cyclical capex trough combined with MATCH Act resolution clears overhang

valuation_summary: Quant DCF yields bear/base/bull IV of $463.57/$665.09/$893.14 (USD) vs current price of $1,459.80; no reasonable assumption set brings IV above current price, with Monte Carlo P(IV > Price) at 0.0%. The stock is materially overvalued despite elite business quality.
compact_checklist: Business: ASML is the sole-source supplier of EUV and High-NA lithography systems (plus a growing EUR 2B+ installed-base service annuity on 6,000+ fielded tools), and TSMC, Samsung, and Intel keep buying because no one else on Earth can print sub-3nm transistors.

Moat: A 100% EUV share locked in by the Zeiss SMT optics partnership (25% owned), the Cymer light source (acquired), ~800 specialist suppliers, 2,000+ EUV patents, and 40 years of co-developed know-how that widens (not resets) with every node - a 10-year-plus structural monopoly.

Economics: ROIC has averaged 41.6% over FY2022-FY2025 against a ~12% WACC, gross margin stepped from 50.5% to 52.8%, FY2025 delivered EUR 11B FCF on EUR 32.7B revenue, and the EUR 38.8B backlog gives two years of forward revenue visibility - textbook economic machine.

Management: Fouquet succeeded Wennink in an orderly internal handover, SBC is only 0.6% of revenue, ~EUR 12B of cumulative buybacks since FY2022 have net-reduced share count, and M&A has been limited to vertical tucks (Cymer, Zeiss SMT stake, HMI) - though dropping quarterly order-intake disclosure in Q1 2026 is a minor transparency regression.

Debt risk: Net cash of EUR 8.9B (EUR 13.3B cash vs EUR 4.4B debt), 97x interest coverage, 0.35x debt/EBITDA, and a customer-prepaid backlog mean ASML could absorb a 30% revenue decline for two years while still funding EUR 4.7B of R&D and never touching the capital markets.

Price: To justify $1,459.80, you must believe ASML sustains 22%+ revenue growth beyond 2030, operating margin expands toward 40%, WACC is sub-10%, and the multiple stays at 22-25x EV/EBITDA - three aggressive priors stacked simultaneously, and Monte Carlo puts P(IV > Price) at 0.0%.

Margin of safety: There is no price MOS - the stock trades 95% above my $750 base IV and 46% above my $1,000 bull IV - so the only cushion is the business itself (monopoly + fortress balance sheet), which protects permanence of capital but not against a repeat of the ~50% drawdowns seen in 2022 and late-2024/early-2025.

Thesis breaker: Sell if High-NA ramp slips 2+ years and customer yields disappoint, if the MATCH Act (or equivalent) passes and cuts China DUV revenue while TSMC publicly defers High-NA beyond 1.4nm, or if gross margin fails to re-expand above 53% by FY2027 as the High-NA learning curve promised.

Current price snapshot $1,459.80 is 2026-04-18 close.
